test_traj.command:
cat > test_alive.ll << 'EOF'
; Test the optimization with signed comparison
define i1 @test_signed(i32 %x, i32 %y, i32 %C) {
  %cmp1 = icmp sgt i32 %x, %y
  %sub = sub nsw i32 %x, %y
  %cmp2 = icmp slt i32 %sub, %C
  %and = and i1 %cmp1, %cmp2
  ret i1 %and
}

; Function with the optimization applied
define i1 @test_signed_opt(i32 %x, i32 %y, i32 %C) {
  %cmp1 = icmp sgt i32 %x, %y
  %sub = sub nsw i32 %x, %y
  %cmp2 = icmp slt i32 %sub, %C
  %and = and i1 %cmp1, %cmp2
  ; If C >= 0, then (x - y) < C is false when x > y
  ; So the result is false
  ret i1 false
}
EOF

echo "Testing with alive-tv..."
alive-tv test_alive.ll test_alive.ll 2>&1 | head -50
test_traj.output:

test_traj.command:
cat > test_edge_case.ll << 'EOF'
; Test edge case: x u> y with samesign, both negative
; x = -10, y = -20, C = 5
; x u> y is true (both negative, same sign)
; x - y = 10
; (x - y) < 5 is false
; So the optimization should say: if x u> y (samesign) and C >= 0, then (x - y) < C is false

define i1 @test_unsigned_samesign(i32 %x, i32 %y, i32 %C) {
  ; Assume x and y have same sign (enforced by some other means)
  %cmp1 = icmp ugt i32 %x, %y
  %sub = sub nsw i32 %x, %y
  %cmp2 = icmp slt i32 %sub, %C
  %and = and i1 %cmp1, %cmp2
  ret i1 %and
}

; With the optimization, if we know C >= 0, the result should be false
define i1 @test_unsigned_samesign_opt(i32 %x, i32 %y, i32 %C) {
  ; Assume C >= 0
  %cmp1 = icmp ugt i32 %x, %y
  %sub = sub nsw i32 %x, %y
  %cmp2 = icmp slt i32 %sub, %C
  %and = and i1 %cmp1, %cmp2
  ret i1 false
}
EOF

echo "Testing edge case..."
alive-tv test_edge_case.ll test_edge_case.ll 2>&1 | head -50
test_traj.output:
